en: Enable the typewriter effect to adjust text speed
Disables floating text animations and the damage vignette effect

Ashvale was a small town. It kept to itself, sustained by a magical core that had burned quietly at its heart for longer than anyone could remember.

Then something began to corrupt it. A creeping thing, slow and certain. They called it the Undoing.

A year ago, {player_name} left to find answers.

Unfortunately, there were no answers to be found. Only the road home.
End of Day Summary for Day {day}
No quests completed today. Tomorrow is a new day!
It's too dark to work. Find your bed.
You should speak\nto the Blacksmith first.
Using tools takes time. Every broken resource node brings you closer to nighttime. When night falls, you will no longer be able to use your tools.
Controls the overall volume of all game audio.
Adjusts the volume of background music.
Adjusts the volume of sound effects like attacks, pickups, and menu interactions.
Switches between fullscreen and windowed display modes.
Scales the size of all menus and interface elements.
When enabled, dialogue text appears one character at a time. When disabled, all text appears instantly.
Controls how quickly characters appear when the typewriter effect is enabled.
Displays a quest arrow that points towards your current objective.
Changes the size of all text throughout the game. Small (16px), Medium (24px), or Large (32px).
When enabled, the camera shakes during combat and other environmental events.
When enabled, floating text animations and the damage vignette effect are turned off for a calmer visual experience.
